entities.name: CO2
entities.description: Carbon dioxide, a specific greenhouse gas measured in the study)

entities.description: The region in Ghana where the study was conducted)

entities.description: The first study site located in the southern zone of Ghana)

entities.name: HEAVY CLAY SOIL

entities.name: LEGON

entities.description: Soil type characterized by sandy texture found at Legon)

entities.name: CATTLE KRAALS
entities.description: A land-use system involving livestock enclosures)

entities.name: NATURAL FORESTS
entities.description: A land-use system consisting of native forest vegetation)

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in cultivated fields at the Kpong site)

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in paddy fields at the Kpong site)

entities.name: WOODLOTS
entities.description: A land-use system consisting of small groups of trees)

entities.name: SOYA BEAN
entities.description: A legume crop grown in cultivated fields at the Legon site)

entities.name: GAS ENTRAPMENT METHOD
entities.description: A technique using PVC chambers to measure gas emissions from soil)

entities.name: SOIL MICROBIAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The biological processes of microorganisms within the soil)

entities.name: ROOT RESPIRATION
entities.description: The process of gas exchange and CO2 production by plant roots)

entities.name: SOIL TEXTURE
entities.description: The relative proportion of sand, silt, and clay in the soil)

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal state of the soil influencing gas production)

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water present in the soil influencing gas production)

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide)

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit area)

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food)
